Resolved by CodeRabbit autofix in commit 287659ce9349 on PR #2774. The 'Killing a wedged holder' section in site/docs/contributing/pr-prep.md now leads with the lsof -t recipe (with empty-output guard) instead of pkill -P. Adds explicit explanation of why pkill -P is insufficient (only direct children, not full process tree). Also references holomush-71zq.12 for the future setsid-based design fix that would obsolete this docs-only mitigation. Duplicate of holomush-gfo6.28 (CLOSED). Same flake, same site (plugins/core-scenes/store_integration_test.go:1184 ListParticipants ordering), same 2026-05-23 timeframe — re-discovered from the hryj live-sandbox log-review batch without realizing the gfo6 nanosecond-timestamp epic already owned it. Root cause is the gfo6 BIGINT-ns migration (gfo6.3/gfo6.9), not the hryj batch. Production fix is in flight in PR #4198 (gfo6.16/iter-18): three deterministic ORDER BY tiebreakers + clock-domain harmonization of CreateWithOwner to SQL-side EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM NOW())*1e9. The clock-domain fix (not the tiebreaker) is what actually guarantees owner-first — a character_id tiebreaker only resolves exact ties, not the Go/PG clock-drift inversion that was the true root cause. World-side mirror tracked by gfo6.31; optional forced-tie regression-test follow-up filed separately.

Shipped via PR #4234. Cursor-bounded backfill: ControlFrame.attach_moment_ms (carried on REPLAY_COMPLETE) + QueryStreamHistoryRequest.not_after_ms (inclusive boundary) plumbed end-to-end. Invalid finding — []string is the CORRECT type, not *[]Participant. (1) The A3 proto contract (already landed, holomush-5rh.20.3) uses 'repeated string participants_snapshot' for both GetPublishedSceneResponse and the PUBLIC GetPublicSceneArchiveResponse; a []Participant struct would mismatch the locked wire contract. (2) Privacy: the public archive MUST expose only character NAMES, never IDs/metadata (spec §3.1 description: 'frozen list of character names visible to public') — []string enforces this by construction; a richer struct risks leaking IDs publicly. (3) Spec §3.1 line 69 type annotation '*[]Participant' is the inaccurate side and is being corrected to []string. Resolved in the A4 commit + spec update. Bead was auto-created by a read-only reviewer (should not mutate state).

Duplicate of holomush-rsoe6.17 (filed by the spec-compliance reviewer for the same SESSION_NOT_FOUND deferred-reattach finding). Resolution decided as comment-only (terminal client re-auth on reap is intentional design); see rsoe6.17 closure.

Resolved by design decision (user-approved): SESSION_NOT_FOUND on re-Subscribe is intentionally terminal. The gateway StreamEvents carries only the session token, not the character_id that SelectCharacter requires, so in-band reattach is not possible without a wire-contract change; the client (which holds both credentials) re-authenticates instead. Critically, this branch does NOT fire on the core-RESTART survival case rsoe6.10 targets — there the session row + durable consumer survive and re-Subscribe resumes transparently. It fires only on genuine reap (past reattach TTL), where client re-auth is the correct recovery. handler.go comment reworded (rsoe6.10 commit) to state this accurately; no dangling follow-up. Spec §7's in-band reattach is superseded by this scoping for the web gateway.

Fixed in code (not just documented): the reconnect ceiling is now PER-OUTAGE, not absolute-from-stream-start. runSubscribeOnce returns (breakCause, opened bool); the outer StreamEvents loop resets outageDeadline (and backoff) to zero on every successful (re)open, and lazily starts a fresh time.Now().Add(ceiling) only when a new outage begins. So a long-healthy stream that later hits a core blip gets a full fresh ceiling — the I-SURV-4 bound is per-outage as intended, not total stream lifetime. Regression test TestStreamEventsReconnectCeilingIsPerOutageNotStreamLifetime added (forces break-time > ceiling-from-open; fails pre-fix, passes post-fix). Landed in the rsoe6.10 commit.

Web gateway survives a core-stream break: StreamEvents restructured into an outer reconnect loop + runSubscribeOnce()(breakCause,opened). On core break: hold client, emit RECONNECTING, backoff+re-Subscribe (JetStream durable resume), dedup redelivery by event id, emit RECONNECTED. Per-outage reconnect ceiling (default 2m; outageDeadline+backoff reset on each successful re-open). Per-attempt subCtx prevents recv-goroutine leak; outer defer Disconnect fires once. SESSION_NOT_FOUND terminal (client re-auths; fires only on reap not restart). Self-verified: build EXIT 0, full ./internal/web/ 228 tests 0 fail under -race, scope=internal/web only. Two-stage review READY (spec-compliance + code-reviewer, 0 blocking). Review-filed findings resolved: rsoe6.17 (SESSION_NOT_FOUND terminal — resolved-by-design), rsoe6.18 (per-outage ceiling — fixed-in-code). Committed pvmf25.

Recv goroutine preserves holomush-umxj local-capture data-race discipline. Self-verified: build EXIT 0, full ./internal/telnet/ 102 tests 0 fail under -race, scope=internal/telnet only (2 files). Fixed (P0). SESSION_NOT_FOUND classification was dead: server Subscribe returned a bare oops (grpc surfaced codes.Unknown) AND client.Subscribe wrapped all errors as RPC_FAILED. Fixed at both layers + both gateways: server.go subscribeSessionNotFound() routes via authFailureToStatus -> codes.Unauthenticated (both SESSION_NOT_FOUND return sites); client.go TranslateSubscribeErr() maps codes.NotFound/Unauthenticated -> oops SESSION_NOT_FOUND else RPC_FAILED. Telnet classifies on the RECV path (lastSubscribeErr+subscribeErrIsTerminal); web classifies on BOTH the synchronous Subscribe return AND the recv path (subscribeRecvErrIsTerminal via status.Code, since server-streaming defers the error to first Recv). Verified: build EXIT0; task test -race ./internal/grpc/ ./internal/telnet/ ./internal/web/ = 725 tests 0 fail 0 races. Gateway grpc→connect status translation landed: a unary connect interceptor (internal/web/status_interceptor.go, mounted on the production NewWebServiceHandler) walks the error chain (errors.As: *connect.Error pass-through first, then interface{GRPCStatus()} through the oops Unwrap chain) and re-emits oops-wrapped grpc status errors as *connect.Error via grpcToConnectCode (PermissionDenied/NotFound/Unauthenticated/... ; default→CodeInternal not Unknown), using st.Message() only. So the SceneAccessService guest gate (PermissionDenied) + unowned-char NotFound now reach the browser as distinct connect codes instead of collapsing to Unknown/500. 5 unit + httptest-roundtrip tests (incl. a no-leak opacity assertion). code review READY (no blockers). OPACITY: NET IMPROVEMENT — pre-change connect-go surfaced the FULL oops chain text as the Unknown wire message; post-change only st.Message() (a strict subset, oops wrapper stripped); reviewer surveyed every core grpc handler — none newly surface sensitive inner text. Resolved via option (b)-reconciliation. INV-CRYPTO-117 clause 3 ('subsystem MUST start when RekeyManager==nil') was not merely unbound but FALSE: INV-CRYPTO-119 (KEK-mandatory boot, activation epic 5rh.8.29.12) makes KEK-less boot fatal (BOOT_KEK_REQUIRED, core.go:750); provisionBootKEKProvider never returns nil-without-error so RekeyManager is guaranteed non-nil in prod; the integration harness provisions a KEK (integrationtest/crypto.go:88) so no test Starts the subsystem KEK-less. Rewrote the 117 summary to drop the contradictory boot clause, keep the genuinely-bound wiring lockstep + nil-passthrough-OPTIONS guarantee (still covered by sub_grpc_test.go), and cross-ref INV-119. Regenerated invariants.md; meta-tests + lint green; crypto-reviewer READY.
